question.text: Which of the following must be included in the Public Announcement for buyback as per Schedule II?

answer.text: Names of institutional shareholders who have pre-committed to tender
Three-year financial projections approved by auditors
Promoter and director shareholding details along with 6-month purchase/sale details with prices

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Schedule II mandates that the PA must include promoter/director shareholding details and the details of purchases and sales of shares by promoters/directors in the preceding 6 months along with prices paid/received. **The Trap:** "Projected post-buyback share price" sounds like useful investor information, but projecting share prices is speculative and is never required in regulatory disclosures. "Three-year financial projections" are also not required. **Cross-Exam Context:** Promoter trading disclosure requirements are tested across multiple NISM exams including Series IX (Merchant Banking) and Series VIII (Equity Derivatives) in insider trading contexts. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** The 6-month trading disclosure helps investors assess whether promoters have been accumulating or disposing shares before the buyback — always verify this data independently.
